cards.front: What is the primary goal of the K-Means algorithm?
cards.back: To identify clusters or groups in a dataset

cards.front: How does the K-Means algorithm handle noise in the dataset?
cards.back: By assigning the noise points to the closest centroid

cards.front: What is the purpose of normalisation in the K-Means algorithm?
cards.back: To scale the features of the dataset to have similar magnitudes

cards.front: What is the benefit of using a better weight update rule in the K-Means Neural Network?
cards.back: Improved convergence to the optimal solution

cards.front: What does Comp stand for in the context of the K-Means algorithm?
